<features>
- Spiky, pointed silvery-blue bracts radiating outwards from the bloom
- Rounded, textured bumpy olive-gray central flower head
- Green sturdy stem supporting the main flower cluster
</features>
<kb>
- IF flower has spiky silvery-blue radiating bracts and a rounded bumpy central flower head THEN class 34
- IF flower has clusters of soft pink/white ruffled petals with darker pink central markings THEN class 71
- IF flower has bright orange or red cup-shaped ruffled petals THEN class 88
- IF flower has large broad purple/magenta petals with pale central stamens THEN class 81
</kb>
<rule_check>
The target image matches the first rule exactly: it has the distinct spiky silvery-blue pointed bracts surrounding a rounded, bumpy central flower head, paired with a green stem, which matches the visual patterns seen in the earlier class 34 examples.
</rule_check>

<response>34</response>

- Single large radially symmetric flower with broad pointed petals
- Petals have pink longitudinal streaking over a white base
- Prominent central stamen/reproductive cluster at the flower center
</features>
<kb>
- IF [flower has spiky globe bloom with silvery-blue bracts] THEN [class 34]
- IF [flower has bright orange/red curved petals with central yellow trumpet structure] THEN [class 88]
- IF [cluster of small pink/lavender flowers with dark pink central throats] THEN [class 71]
- IF [single large flower with broad pointed petals with pink veining on white base, prominent central stamen cluster] THEN [class 81]
</kb>
<rule_check>
The target image matches the IF rule for class 81 exactly: it is a single large flower with broad petals marked with pink longitudinal stripes on a white base, with a prominent central stamen cluster, matching the visual features of the previously labeled class 81 Clematis flowers.
</rule_check>

<response>81</response>
